ux_issues.description: AI called create_itinerary_items twice in a single turn — first call succeeded (2 items), second call was a duplicate that was correctly rejected with 'already_exists'. This results in an awkward doubled response where the AI confirms the items twice.
ux_issues.suggestion: Investigate why the model is making a duplicate tool call in the same turn. The dedup logic in the backend works correctly, but the AI should not be calling the same tool with the same arguments twice.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items twice with identical arguments in the same SendMessage turn. The backend correctly deduplicated (returned already_exists on second call), but this wastes a tool loop iteration and produces a repetitive response.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Step 6: planning message to add Kinkaku-ji and Fushimi Inari to day 1

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: All 14 lifecycle steps passed cleanly. COALESCE partial updates work correctly — updating title preserves description and vice versa. Status transitions preserve all fields. Cascade deletion properly removes trip, bookings, and itinerary. Share token lifecycle works: enable returns public data, disable revokes access immediately. The only issue observed was the AI making a duplicate tool call in a single turn, which the backend handled gracefully. Booking parsing extracted all key fields from the Delta flight confirmation correctly.
